title: Open Recipe Standard
$comment: Normative JSON Schema for the Open Recipe Standard: a portable, vendor-neutral format for encoding recipes for interchange between applications and libraries. Mass, duration, temperature, and count use SI / metric conventions (unit_system metric). Volume may use metric units (ml, cl, L) or US customary kitchen measures (tsp, tbsp, cup) with unit_system us_customary. Optional metadata includes categories, cor, cuisine, tags, dietary/allergen hints, skill level, meal type, equipment, and active/prep/total times. For ingredient amounts, unit follows the allowlist for each quantity_kind. For base_yield when quantity_kind is count, unit is a free-form label. Provisional fields use keys matching ^X-.

recipe_uuid.description: Stable identifier (e.g. vendor prefix + unique id) for this recipe instance.

recipe_authors.description: People credited as creating or writing this recipe (the culinary author(s)). Use for originals and general attribution. When the text comes from a book or site, source_authors may match or overlap; source_authors ties authorship to that publication.

categories.description: High-level categorical tags for filtering and UI. Examples: taste (sweet, savoury, spicy, mild, umami); course (appetizer, starter, mains, side, dessert, snack); meal time (breakfast, brunch, lunch, dinner); style (comfort_food, kid_friendly, quick, make_ahead, one_pot, baked, grilled, fried, steamed, raw). Open vocabulary; keep spelling consistent in a dataset.

cor.description: Country of recipe (COR): countries or regions associated with the dish’s origin or style (e.g. France, Italy, Australia, China). Use display names or ISO 3166 codes; stay consistent within a dataset.

cuisine.description: Culinary tradition or style (often broader than cor), e.g. Italian, Mexican, Japanese, Middle Eastern, Nordic, Southern US. Open vocabulary.

tags.description: Search and discovery keywords: ingredients, techniques, themes (e.g. chocolate, summer, gluten-free-baking). Complements categories.

dietary.description: Dietary suitability labels, e.g. vegetarian, vegan, pescatarian, halal, kosher, gluten-free, dairy-free, nut-free, low-sodium, keto, paleo. Open vocabulary; not a substitute for allergen declarations.

allergens.description: Allergens present in the recipe (for consumer safety). Use consistent tokens in a dataset, e.g. milk, eggs, fish, crustaceans, molluscs, tree_nuts, peanuts, wheat, soy, sesame, mustard, lupin, celery, sulphites (align with local labeling rules as needed).

skill_level.description: Approximate difficulty for a typical home cook.

meal_type.description: One or more meal or eating contexts, each chosen from the fixed MealType list.

prep_time.description: Active preparation time (chopping, mixing) before cooking; quantity_kind duration.

cook_time.description: Cooking or baking time (stove, oven, grill); quantity_kind duration.

total_time.description: End-to-end time including prep, cook, and mandatory passive steps (rest, chill) if the author includes them; quantity_kind duration.

equipment.description: Tools or appliances assumed, e.g. oven, microwave, refrigerator, stand mixer, food processor, immersion blender, grill, wok.

base_yield.description: What the written ingredient amounts produce. Only count, mass, or volume (not temperature or duration). For count output, unit is any non-empty label (e.g. cookies, muffins). For mass or volume, unit is a fixed code: metric (g, ml, …) or, for volume only, US customary tsp, tbsp, or cup (with unit_system us_customary). To scale: multiply each ingredient amount by (target_yield_amount / base_yield.amount) when comparable, or by an explicit scale factor.

ingredients.description: Ingredients in order of use; repeat lines when used at different stages. Each amount is for base_yield unless scaled at display time.

oven_fan.description: Convection setting; if omitted, implementations may treat as Off.

oven_temp.description: Starting oven temperature; quantity_kind temperature; unit C (degrees Celsius).

oven_time.description: Overall oven duration; quantity_kind duration; metric time units (min, s, h, etc.).

source_url.description: URL this recipe was taken from, if applicable.

source_authors.description: Author(s) credited on the external source (book, site, show), not the person who keyed the recipe into an app. Often aligns with recipe_authors when the source is the canonical attribution.

SkillLevel.description: Relative difficulty for a typical home cook.

MealType.description: Meal or eating context: main mealtimes, snack/dessert, tea, first course, or side dish.

QuantityKind.description: mass vs volume vs count; temperature (°C) and duration (s, min, h) for oven fields. Volume may use metric (ml, L) or US customary kitchen units (tsp, tbsp, cup); volume is never mass.

UnitSystem.description: "metric" for SI / metric units and fixed count labels. "us_customary" only when quantity_kind is volume and unit is tsp, tbsp, or cup (US kitchen measures).

Measurement.description: A quantity with explicit kind and unit_system. For volume, either metric units (ml, cl, L) with unit_system metric, or US customary tsp, tbsp, cup with unit_system us_customary. Other kinds use unit_system metric. Field unit must match quantity_kind (see allOf).

amount.description: Numeric amount in the given unit.

unit.description: Unit code for this quantity_kind (see allOf allowlists). Volume may be metric or US customary (tsp, tbsp, cup).

oneOf.description: US customary kitchen volume (teaspoon, tablespoon, US cup).

DurationMeasurement.description: A duration-only measurement for prep, cook, or total time. Uses quantity_kind duration and units ms, s, min, or h.

YieldMeasurement.description: How much recipe output the written amounts represent: count, mass, or volume only. For count, unit is a free-form label (what is being counted). For mass or volume, unit follows the same rules as Measurement (metric or, for volume, US customary tsp/tbsp/cup with unit_system us_customary).

oneOf.description: Output counted in discrete units; unit is any non-empty custom label (e.g. cookies, egg rolls, slices).

amount.description: How many of the thing named by unit (e.g. 24 when unit is cookies).

unit.description: Human-readable name for one counted item or batch (plural is fine), not limited to a fixed vocabulary.

oneOf.description: Output as total mass or volume using metric units or US customary volume units (see Measurement).

quantity_kind.description: Total mass or total volume of the recipe output.

IngredientSansSubstitutions.description: Ingredient line without nested substitutions (used inside substitutions arrays).

amount.description: Amount for base_yield; scale with the recipe when changing batch size.

processing.description: Tags like whole, minced, large dice.

usda_num.description: USDA Standard Reference index key for nutrition lookup.

Ingredient.description: Full ingredient line including optional substitutions.

substitutions.description: Same shape as an ingredient but without substitutions on nested items.

Haccp.description: HACCP annotation: either control_point or critical_control_point, not both.
